title: The End of
wikitext:
[]: The End of...             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Tue Jul 19, 2011 7:53 pm The last scene of EoE, One More Final is but one side to a big coin (at least in my view). Considering where it comes from, I don't think it should be of any surprise. When I saw the comparison for the first time, I was reminded of this thread , where I tried to establish the meanings of sun and moon duality within Evangelion. Evidently, I was not far off. There was something there after all, but the real key to the door was in another series. I don't think this comparison has been made before, if it has then sorry for wasting space. Also, thread contains spoilers. The first side we already know...(Warning: lot's of images) SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Jul 19, 2011 8:42 pm Woah. That's actually really cool; if it's been posted about before, this is the first I've seen it, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jul 20, 2011 2:15 am Oh Anno, you and your self-references. :3

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:06 am Very interesting. Something I haven't seen before at least. Thank you, Ironfoot !

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:53 am Woah. That's quite a catch. Nice work! The similarities are certainly there.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Wed Jul 20, 2011 11:52 pm See this is why this show is a masterpiece because even after 16 years, there is still so many thing that have gone unnoticed. Thank You Ironfoot! Your research is top notch!

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Thu Jul 21, 2011 4:08 pm Thanks guys. :) There are other minor details too, like Shinji looking at the moon while Nadia is looking at the sun, and of course the footprints out of the water. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Fri Jul 22, 2011 12:41 am I stumbled across a site a few weeks ago that catalogues many of the similarities between Evangelion and Nadia . I thought it was interesting, but forgot to share it. Have you seen it before, Ironfoot? http://www.thesecretofbluewater.com/nadieva-i.htm The site screams, "Hey! I coded this in 2002 and then forgot to update it!" and the spoiler-warning-popup gets a little annoying, but it's a cool resource of images. Heh. ;) Nice catch with the EoE images... I don't think that site has them. And it's an interesting thought that Anno didn't make EoE (as debunked rumor has it) as an FFFFUUUUUUU to EoTV fans, but as a way to remdy the errors and trangressions he thought he saw in Nadia . I haven't seen that series before; it looks like one I'll have to check out...

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Fri Jul 22, 2011 3:13 am Damn, I didn't know all about this. Thanks for bringing this up, not everyone realized that some chunks of Eva came from Nadia.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sat Jul 23, 2011 11:29 am Fireand'chutes77 wrote: I stumbled across a site a few weeks ago that catalogues many of the similarities between Evangelion and Nadia . I thought it was interesting, but forgot to share it. Have you seen it before, Ironfoot? http://www.thesecretofbluewater.com/nadieva-i.htm

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sun Jul 24, 2011 6:54 pm I was about to say that Nadia inspired End of Eva to use those shots, but nope... just found out that it was made 5 years before Evangelion was made... interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by ericm » Sun Jul 24, 2011 9:11 pm That site does a pretty great job with the comparisons. I watched Nadia after watching Eva in full, and I personally didn't pick out any comparisons until the show took a major turn and the comparisons at that point become obvious. Or rather, they start to allude to some things when the crew of the Nautilus goes to that graveyard thing, but then go away from all the more interesting stuff and return to the 'normalness' of the series, but come back later full on, after all the 'bad' non-Anno episodes. Frankly, Nadia is not a very good series. I'd say 90% of it is as bad as most mainstream anime I've seen (a bad thing). I think it's initially interesting, just runs of steam very quickly, gains some definite momentum when you realize there is a lot more to the Nautilus crew than meets the eye, but again, they really get away from all that and focus on many trivialities, on the "good guy, bad guy" dynamic, and lots of bad action scenes throughout. By the time it does decide to get interesting again, the last 5 eps or so, the series has already majorly run of steam. Alas, at that point you are bombarded with fragmented ideas that seem straight out of Eva, if you only hadn't known this was done before Eva. Let me summarize; For a fan of Eva, I think it's ALMOST worth it to suffer through this series to see all those Eva precursor-ideas at the end. Almost. Though that site listing the similarities does illuminate a lot of things I would not have picked out on my own.
